en.extract:
The black-chinned babbler (Cyanoderma pyrrhops) is a babbler species in the family Timaliidae. It occurs in the foothills of the Himalayas from the Murree Hills in Pakistan to eastern Nepal. It inhabits subtropical and temperate forest at 245–2,750 m (804–9,022 ft) altitudes. It is terrestrial. It is listed as Least Concern on the IUCN Red List.
It is biscuit-coloured, has a black chin and lores and a buffy grey crown. It is 10 cm (3.9 in) long and weighs 8–12 g (0.28–0.42 oz).
It is omnivorous.
Stachyris pyrrhops was the scientific name proposed by Edward Blyth in 1844 who described a greenish olivaceous babbler with a black chin and black lores from Nepal.
It was later placed in the genus Stachyridopsis.


References


External links
BirdLife International (2019). "Black-chinned Babbler Cyanoderma pyrrhops".

The '''black-chinned babbler''' ('''''Cyanoderma pyrrhops''''') is a babbler species in the family [[Timaliidae]]. It occurs in the foothills of the [[Himalayas]] from the [[Murree Hills]] in Pakistan to eastern Nepal. It inhabits subtropical and [[temperate forest]] at {{convert|245–2750|m|abbr=on}} altitudes. It is terrestrial.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Sohil |first1=Asha |last2=Sharma |first2=Neeraj |date=August 2020 |title=Bird diversity and distribution in mosaic landscapes around Jammu, Jammu & Kashmir |journal=Acta Ecologica Sinica |volume=40 |issue=4 |pages=323–338 |doi=10.1016/j.chnaes.2020.02.005 |bibcode=2020AcEcS..40..323S |issn=1872-2032}}</ref> It is listed as [[Least Concern]] on the [[IUCN Red List]].<ref name=iucn/>

It is biscuit-coloured, has a black chin and [[lore (anatomy)|lores]] and a buffy grey crown. It is {{convert|10|cm|abbr=on}} long and weighs {{convert|8–12|g|abbr=on}}.<ref name=HBoW2016>{{cite book |year=2016 |editor=del Hoyo, J. |editor2=Elliott, A. |editor3=Sargatal, J. |editor4=Christie, D. A. |editor5=de Juana, E. |title=Handbook of the Birds of the World |publisher=Lynx Edicions |location=Barcelona |volume=2: Passerines |last1=Collar |first1=N. J. |last2=Robson |first2=C. |chapter=Black-chinned Babbler (''Cyanoderma pyrrhops'') |chapter-url=https://www.hbw.com/species/black-chinned-babbler-cyanoderma-pyrrhops}}</ref>

It is omnivorous.<ref name=":0" />

''Stachyris pyrrhops'' was the scientific name proposed by [[Edward Blyth]] in 1844 who [[scientific description|described]] a greenish olivaceous babbler with a black chin and black [[Lore (anatomy)|lores]] from Nepal.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Blyth |first1=E. |year=1844 |title=Appendix for Mr. Blyth's report for December Meeting 1842 |journal=The Journal of the Asiatic Society of Bengal |volume=13 |issue=149 |pages=361–395 |url=https://archive.org/details/journalofasiatic131asia/page/379}}</ref>
It was later placed in the genus ''Stachyridopsis''.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Moyle |first1=R. G. |last2=Andersen |first2=M. J. |last3=Oliveros |first3=C. H. |last4=Steinheimer |first4=F. D. |last5=Reddy |first5=S. |year=2012 |title=Phylogeny and Biogeography of the Core Babblers (Aves: Timaliidae) |journal=Systematic Biology |volume=61 |issue=4 |pages=631–651 |doi=10.1093/sysbio/sys027 |pmid=22328569 |doi-access=free}}</ref><ref name=HBoW2016/>

zh.extract:
黑颏穗鹛（台湾作紅嘴穗鶥）（学名：Cyanoderma pyrrhops），是画眉科的一种，分布于印度、尼泊尔和巴基斯坦。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
黑颏穗鹛的平均体重约为10.5克。栖息地包括温带森林、亚热带或热带的湿润低地林和温带疏灌丛。


特征
黑颏穗鹛体重约9.59克，翼长约52.5毫米，嘴峰长约13.8毫米，喙宽度约2.3毫米，喙厚度约3.1毫米，跗蹠长约18.6毫米，尾长约51.5毫米。黑颏穗鹛在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在半开放的高大树木组成的森林中，食性为肉食性，主要食物来源是陆生无脊椎动物。


分布
喜马拉雅山脉（克什米尔至尼泊尔中部）。


参考文献
Stachyris pyrrhops. BirdLife.org.   [2011-06-27].
